For instance, a native is tabooed for several days when his hair is cut; when he is tattooed; when he is building a canoe, or a house; when he is seriously ill, and when he is dead. If excessive consumption threatens to exterminate the fish of a river, or ruin the early crop of sweet potatoes, these things are put under the protection of the taboo. The landing is the founding event of the whole novel: from this violent arrival springs the colonists' long labour of survival and the island's slowly unfolding mystery.
